User Story: As a private journal owner, I want protected pages to redirect me to login when I am unauthenticated so my journal stays private.
Current Behaviour: Unauthenticated page requests redirect to /login with next set to the originally requested path unless public read is enabled.
Expected Behaviour: Unauthenticated users cannot view private pages; authenticated users land back on their intended path.
UX Issues: No major issue found.
Product Issues: Shared-token auth is simple and private, but not multi-user.
Technical Debt: Cookie behaviour is integration-dependent and not covered by automated tests.
Edge Cases: Missing or invalid token; public-read mode; next path preservation.
Success Metric: Unauthenticated private page requests redirect reliably.
Errors Found: Not browser-tested with real cookies because no JOURNAL_ACCESS_TOKEN/Supabase test environment was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to enter my access token so I can open the app.
Current Behaviour: Submitting a valid token to /api/login sets an httpOnly cookie and routes to the next path; invalid tokens show an error.
Expected Behaviour: Login should feel immediate, private, and recover gracefully from invalid tokens.
UX Issues: The form is appropriately sparse; error copy is clear.
Product Issues: No onboarding beyond token entry, which fits the private-product strategy.
Technical Debt: No automated login integration test.
Edge Cases: Invalid token; network failure; empty token; preserved next route.
Success Metric: Valid token opens the app without repeated login prompts.
Errors Found: Not integration-tested against a real JOURNAL_ACCESS_TOKEN in this environment.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to change token/logout so another token can be entered.
Current Behaviour: Clicking Change token POSTs /api/logout, clears the auth cookie, and sends the browser to /login.
Expected Behaviour: The user can leave the private session or switch token intentionally.
UX Issues: Icon-only action is quiet but relies on title/aria-label for meaning.
Product Issues: This supports privacy without adding account complexity.
Technical Debt: No browser cookie test.
Edge Cases: Logout API failure; stale cookie; redirect after logout.
Success Metric: Logout returns the user to token entry.
Errors Found: Not browser-tested with real cookies because no auth env was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to type today's note and save it with Enter or Ctrl/Cmd+S so today's journal entry is recorded quickly.
Current Behaviour: A non-empty trimmed draft upserts today's UTC entry, reloads the full entry list, recomputes streak state, focuses/selects the input, and unlocks the daily quote.
Expected Behaviour: Capture should be the fastest path in the app and should never ask for unnecessary decisions.
UX Issues: Very strong: the first screen is the actual capture surface.
Product Issues: UTC day semantics may surprise users outside UTC if they expect local-day journaling.
Technical Debt: No automated client interaction test for save/focus behaviour.
Edge Cases: Whitespace-only draft; unchanged draft; network failure; UTC date boundary.
Success Metric: Time from open to saved entry stays under a few seconds.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found in this pass.
Retest Result: Pass via type/build checks

User Story: As a journaler, I want to edit previous entries inline so I can correct a day without opening a separate screen.
Current Behaviour: Blurring an edited row saves trimmed non-empty changed text; Enter commits by blurring; failed saves revert visible text.
Expected Behaviour: Editing should feel local, reversible, and low-friction.
UX Issues: Inline editing is efficient but hidden until the user tries it.
Product Issues: No history/versioning for accidental edits.
Technical Debt: No dedicated tests for blur/Enter/revert behaviour.
Edge Cases: Empty edit; failed save; very long text; keyboard-only editing.
Success Metric: Corrections save without navigating away.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to delete an entry and undo accidental deletion so I can remove mistakes safely.
Current Behaviour: Delete optimistically removes the row, calls DELETE /api/entries/:day, and shows an Undo toast that restores the entry.
Expected Behaviour: Deletion should avoid blocking dialogs while still making recovery obvious.
UX Issues: Undo is calmer than confirmation and fits the app tone.
Product Issues: Toast timeout is the only recovery window.
Technical Debt: No automated optimistic-delete/undo test.
Edge Cases: Delete current-day entry; API failure; undo after search; repeated deletes.
Success Metric: Accidental deletes can be restored from the toast.
Errors Found: Tracker previously described obsolete confirmation-dialog behaviour.
Retest Result: Pass via documentation update

User Story: As a journaler, I want to search previous entries so I can find memories by text.
Current Behaviour: Opening search switches to Journal and debounces GET /api/entries?q=...; Escape clears/closes.
Expected Behaviour: Search should be available on demand without becoming permanent furniture.
UX Issues: Reveal-on-demand keeps Today quiet.
Product Issues: Search results replace the main entries list rather than a separate results mode.
Technical Debt: No automated debounce/search UI test.
Edge Cases: Slash inside inputs; Escape close; no matches; large journals.
Success Metric: User can find an old entry by keyword quickly.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found in this pass.
Retest Result: Pass via type/build checks

User Story: As a keyboard-oriented user, I want shortcuts for save and search so common actions are fast.
Current Behaviour: Ctrl/Cmd+S saves the draft; / focuses search only when focus is not already in editable controls.
Expected Behaviour: Shortcuts should speed up power users without hijacking typing.
UX Issues: Good balance: shortcuts exist but are not advertised as clutter.
Product Issues: Shortcut discoverability is low by design.
Technical Debt: No keyboard E2E test.
Edge Cases: Editable targets; buttons; contenteditable rows; browser save prevention.
Success Metric: Shortcuts work without interfering with normal text entry.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found in this pass.
Retest Result: Pass via type/build checks

User Story: As a user, I want to export all entries to JSON so I can back up my journal.
Current Behaviour: Export downloads verum-export.json from /api/entries/export; monthly backup reminders can deep-link to ?export=1.
Expected Behaviour: Backup should be private, explicit, and fast.
UX Issues: Icon-only export is calm but easy to miss.
Product Issues: No cloud backup; intentional simplicity.
Technical Debt: Download flow not covered by automated browser test.
Edge Cases: Popup/download restrictions; empty journal; export deep link; network failure.
Success Metric: User can produce a local backup without database access.
Errors Found: Not browser-download-tested because no running authenticated Supabase environment was provided.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to see my current streak so I know whether I have logged today.
Current Behaviour: The app computes streak client-side from entries and renders a quiet current-streak line with logged/not-logged state.
Expected Behaviour: Streak should motivate without dominating the reflection experience.
UX Issues: Quiet treatment avoids turning the journal into a game dashboard.
Product Issues: Streaks can motivate retention but may add pressure for a reflective product.
Technical Debt: Date logic is split between client streak and notification streak helpers.
Edge Cases: UTC/local-day differences; empty history; deletion of today; duplicate days.
Success Metric: The user knows whether today's record is alive.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want a quote after logging today so journaling is rewarded by a daily prompt.
Current Behaviour: QuoteBox appears only after today's entry exists or is saved; it fetches /api/quotes/daily and supports voting/deletion.
Expected Behaviour: Reward should come after reflection, not before it.
UX Issues: The gated quote correctly keeps writing first.
Product Issues: Quote reward can become the reason to log, which is useful but should remain secondary.
Technical Debt: Supabase RPC availability is required and not locally simulated.
Edge Cases: No quote available; RPC failure; already logged today on load.
Success Metric: Users perceive a small reward after logging.
Errors Found: Not integration-tested because the Supabase RPC verum_pick_daily_quote is not available locally.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to thumbs up/down a quote so the app can learn core quotes.
Current Behaviour: Thumb buttons POST delta 1 or -1, update score server-side, and return quote with is_core based on threshold.
Expected Behaviour: Voting should be one tap and never interrupt reading.
UX Issues: Actions reveal on hover/focus and remain visible enough on touch.
Product Issues: Voting has no visible explanation of what core means, which may be acceptable for a personal tool.
Technical Debt: No rate limit or per-user vote history; shared owner model reduces risk.
Edge Cases: Repeated votes; failed request; quote deleted while voting.
Success Metric: Quote scores reflect the owner's preference over time.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to delete a quote and undo accidental deletion so unwanted quotes stop appearing.
Current Behaviour: Delete removes the quote, refreshes the daily quote, and offers an Undo toast that recreates the quote.
Expected Behaviour: Deletion should be fast and recoverable.
UX Issues: Undo matches entry deletion and avoids a jarring dialog.
Product Issues: Undo recreates the quote but may not preserve original score/history.
Technical Debt: No automated undo test.
Edge Cases: Daily-pick cascade; undo after duplicate quote; failed refresh.
Success Metric: Unwanted quotes can be removed without fear.
Errors Found: Tracker previously described obsolete confirmation-dialog behaviour.
Retest Result: Pass via documentation update

User Story: As an integrator, I want quote list/create endpoints so quotes can be curated outside the UI.
Current Behaviour: GET /api/quotes lists all/core/regular quotes; POST /api/quotes creates idempotently by text+author and returns is_core.
Expected Behaviour: API curation should be stable even without an admin UI.
UX Issues: Keeping quote creation out of the main UI preserves product calm.
Product Issues: API-only curation is a hidden maintenance workflow.
Technical Debt: No API contract tests.
Edge Cases: Duplicate quote; empty author; core filter; unauthenticated write.
Success Metric: Maintainer can curate quotes without database console edits.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a journaler, I want to see previous entries from this calendar date so the app can create meaningful reflection moments.
Current Behaviour: After load, /api/entries/anniversaries returns prior-year entries for today's month-day; PastEntries renders only when matches exist.
Expected Behaviour: Recall should appear only when meaningful and stay quiet by default.
UX Issues: Excellent retention hook with no new data entry burden.
Product Issues: Only works when the user has multi-year history.
Technical Debt: Supabase date matching is server-dependent and not locally integration-tested.
Edge Cases: Leap day; same day this year; no matches; malformed dates.
Success Metric: Users rediscover old entries without searching.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want a life-calendar visualization so I can see elapsed weeks and weeks with entries.
Current Behaviour: The calendar renders configured lifespan rows, marks past/current weeks, and adds dots for entry weeks.
Expected Behaviour: The life view should provoke reflection without demanding interpretation.
UX Issues: Powerful but dense; kept in a secondary tab, which is right.
Product Issues: May be emotionally heavy for some users; product copy stays minimal.
Technical Debt: The calendar algorithm is complex and not unit-tested in isolation.
Edge Cases: DOB/env missing; 53-week years; mobile widths; reduced motion.
Success Metric: User can understand lifetime context at a glance.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to pin small life markers to specific weeks so the life calendar becomes personal history rather than only anonymous cells.
Current Behaviour: Clicking a calendar cell opens a marker editor with label, accent swatches, Pin, and Remove; markers persist via Supabase.
Expected Behaviour: Marker editing should be clear, compact, and visually stable in light and dark mode.
UX Issues: The inline editor is efficient but needs reliable surface styling and selected-ring contrast.
Product Issues: Markers deepen retention by turning the calendar into personal memory infrastructure.
Technical Debt: The editor referenced an undefined --paper CSS token before this pass.
Edge Cases: Empty label; invalid accent; API failure; marker on compressed week; dark mode.
Success Metric: A marker can be pinned, recognized, edited, and removed without visual glitches.
Errors Found: Marker editor used undefined var(--paper), making background/ring styling invalid.
Retest Result: Pass via type/build checks

User Story: As a user, I want to enable reminders on this device so Verum can nudge me when I want it to.
Current Behaviour: Reminders tab detects browser support/permission/subscription state, subscribes to Web Push, and stores subscriptions server-side.
Expected Behaviour: Setup should be explicit and sparse, with device-level control.
UX Issues: Clear iPhone Home Screen guidance reduces PWA confusion.
Product Issues: Web Push support varies by browser/device; this remains inherently fiddly.
Technical Debt: No browser/device integration tests for notification permission flows.
Edge Cases: Unsupported browser; denied permission; stale subscription; iPhone PWA install requirement.
Success Metric: User can enable and disable reminders on a device.
Errors Found: Not tested against real browser push services in this environment.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want to control daily, rescue, weekly, and backup reminders so nudges stay useful rather than annoying.
Current Behaviour: Preferences supports daily reminder, streak rescue, weekly reflection, monthly backup, times, minimum streak, weekday, and day of month.
Expected Behaviour: Reminder controls should be understandable and should keep defaults sparse.
UX Issues: Comprehensive, but the Reminders tab is now the densest screen in the product.
Product Issues: Four reminder types may be more than the simplest product needs; defaults being off mitigates this.
Technical Debt: No UI tests for optimistic preference rollback.
Edge Cases: Invalid HH:mm; number bounds; failed preference save; approximate cron timing.
Success Metric: Enabled reminders match user intent and remain sparse.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a user, I want reminders to arrive only when relevant so they build trust instead of noise.
Current Behaviour: Cron dispatches daily, rescue, weekly, and backup jobs by ?job=; each job gates eligibility and dedupes deliveries.
Expected Behaviour: Notifications should be timely, relevant, and never duplicated for the same period.
UX Issues: Server-side dedupe is strong; copy is calm.
Product Issues: Hobby-plan cron timing is approximate; UI explains this.
Technical Debt: Requires Vercel cron, VAPID, Supabase, and CRON_SECRET to be configured correctly.
Edge Cases: DST drift; disabled prefs; no entries; sent/failed delivery log; gone subscriptions.
Success Metric: Users receive no more than the intended reminder per period.
Errors Found: Not executed against live Vercel cron/push infrastructure.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As an API caller, I want unauthorized writes blocked and optional public reads supported so privacy settings are consistent.
Current Behaviour: API route handlers call requireAuth; GETs are public only when JOURNAL_PUBLIC_READ=true; mutations require the token cookie.
Expected Behaviour: Privacy rules should be consistent across every endpoint.
UX Issues: Consistent server-side auth keeps the UI simple.
Product Issues: Single-token model means all authenticated access has the same privileges.
Technical Debt: No endpoint-level automated auth matrix.
Edge Cases: Public read mode; unauthenticated mutation; missing env vars.
Success Metric: Unauthenticated writes are blocked everywhere.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found.
Retest Result: Pass via static/code review and production build

User Story: As a maintainer, I want non-interactive validation commands so CI/agents can verify the app without prompts.
Current Behaviour: package.json exposes typecheck and build scripts; no misleading lint alias is advertised.
Expected Behaviour: Validation should be honest and repeatable in agent/CI contexts.
UX Issues: Simple scripts are easy to trust.
Product Issues: No unit/E2E suite yet; type/build are necessary but insufficient.
Technical Debt: Coverage remains mostly static/integration by inspection.
Edge Cases: Missing env during build; TypeScript regressions; Next build errors.
Success Metric: Maintainer can run typecheck/build without interactive prompts.
Errors Found: No code-level errors found in this pass.
